data.narrative:
Vannevar Labs is a Series B defense and security company founded by Brett Granberg and Nini Moorhead, currently valued at $1.5B post-money following $90M in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms across the same sector and stage.

Two primary valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical Series B-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.9x to the $1.5B last post-money valuation, yielding $2.9B. Peer-median benchmarking across five same-sector, same-stage private comparables—Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B)—establishes a median valuation of $1.0B with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The divergence between these methods reflects variance in growth trajectory assumptions relative to peer performance.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $850M to $3.3B, with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits below the median estimate, suggesting potential upside if the company demonstrates growth tracking closer to stage-progression expectations. Key uncertainties include relative product maturity versus peers, revenue trajectory, and capital efficiency in the capital-intensive defense sector.
